title: Follow Us On:
description: NewsWorldGlobalUK NewsCanada NewsIndians AbroadShowsChina NewsUS NewsAdvertisementGlobalRepeated Pak attacks can't mask its internal crisis: Afghanistan vows retaliationAfghanistan criticised Pakistan for carrying out airstrikes on terror targets inside its territory that it said killed dozens of civilians, warning that repeated attacks by Islamabad would not conceal its internal crisis.GlobalHong Kong govt offers to buy ownership of apartments gutted in devastating fireThe government estimates that buying back the rights of some 1,700 units would cost about 6.8 billion Hong Kong dollars (USD 870 million), with about HK$4 billion Hong Kong (USD 512 million) of that sum to be from public funds and the rest to be covered by a relief fund.GlobalMillions of Americans brace for blizzard conditions as storm threatens East CoastThe weather service said 1 to 2 feet (about 30 to 61 centimeters) of snow was possible in many areas as it issued blizzard warnings for New York City and Long Island, Boston and coastal communities in New Jersey, Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Rhode Island and Massachusetts. Flooding was also possible in parts of New York and New Jersey, it said.US NewsLacks legal teeth: Lawyer behind tariff setback flags flaws in Trump's planIndian-origin lawyer Neal Katyal, who successfully challenged Donald Trump's global tariffs in the US Supreme Court, has questioned the US President's plan to use Section 122, saying the law lacks legal force in this case. Section 122 permits a US president to levy tariffs of up to 15% for a maximum of 150 days to tackle balance-of-payments deficits, after which approval from Congress is required.advertisementGlobalDozens killed in Afghanistan as Pakistan launches 'targeted strikes' along borderPakistan's 'targeted strikes' along the Afghan border have allegedly killed at least 17 members of the same family, while Islamabad maintained that the attacks were aimed at militant targets.GlobalPew study finds Hindus lead US religious groups in higher educationSeven-in-ten Hindus (70 per cent) and 65 per cent of Jews have a bachelor's degree or more education; that compares with 35 per cent of US adults overall, according to the RLS study made public on February 19.GlobalUS Ambassador sparks uproar with claim Israel has right to much of Middle EastMike Huckabee's comments sparked immediate backlash from neighbouring Egypt and Jordan, Saudi Arabia, the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation and the League of Arab States, which in separate statements called them extremist, provocative and not in line with the US position.GlobalRenewed student protests sweep Iranian universities as semester opensA video purportedly showed rows of marchers at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology condemning Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as a "murderous leader", and calling for Reza Pahlavi, the exiled son of Iran's toppled shah, to be a new monarch.advertisementGlobalGood to have counterweights in democracies: Macron on US Supreme Court tariff orderFrench President Emmanuel Macron welcomed the US Supreme Court's decision striking down Donald Trump's emergency tariffs, saying democracies need counterweights and rule of law.GlobalSenior Hezbollah leader among 10 killed in Israeli strikes on LebanonAt least 10 people, including a senior Hezbollah leader, were killed in Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon after the Israel Defence Forces said it targeted Hezbollah positions.GlobalIran can sink US aircraft carriers, says Khamenei. What weapon can it use?Iran's Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei, stated the country possesses a dangerous weapon capable of sending US aircraft carriers to the bottom of the sea. While he did not mention the exact weapon, Iran does possess a vast arsenal of hypersonic and subsonic missiles that it could use against US warships, should need arise.GlobalThe Indian-origin lawyer behind Trump's tariff setback in US courtNeal Katyal, a former Acting Solicitor General of the US and author, has emerged as a central figure in the Supreme Court case that struck down Trump's global tariffs. Who is he?advertisementGlobalStay at home, don't interfere: Macron shreds Meloni over French activist's deathA dispute has intensified between French President Emmanuel Macron and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni following the killing of French activist Quentin Deranque, drawing attention to growing ideological divides within Europe.US NewsTrump given options to kill Khamenei and son in targeted Iran strikes: ReportUS President Donald Trump has been briefed on military options that include a targeted strike against Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and his son Mojtaba Khamenei, Axios reported, citing senior administration sources.US NewsUS boy, 11, kills adoptive father for taking away game console, faces adult trialAn 11-year-old Pennsylvania boy shot his adoptive father in the head while he slept after his gaming console was taken away on the night of his birthday.US NewsI can do anything I want: Defiant Trump after US Supreme Court tariff blowPresident Donald Trump said he could do anything he wanted in his first reaction to a landmark ruling by the US Supreme Court on Friday that struck down his global tariffs, dealing a major blow to his second-term trade agenda.44:59GlobalAsos founder Quentin Griffiths dies after fall from 17th floor of Thailand homeQuentin Griffiths, 58, co-founder of British fashion retailer Asos, has died after falling from his 17th-floor apartment in Thailand's Pattaya on February 9. CCTV footage and an autopsy found no evidence of foul play, and police believe the death was a suicide.US NewsWho are US Supreme Court judges who ruled 6‑3 against Trump's global tariffs?Donald Trump's global tariffs have been struck down by the Supreme Court. Here are the judges who ruled them unconstitutional and beyond the president's powers.US NewsFools, lapdogs, disgrace to nation: Trump's all-out attack on court after tariff blowCertain members of the court are nothing but fools and lapdogs for the RINOs and the radical left,  deeply unpatriotic and disloyal to the Constitution, regardless of any other considerations, Trump alleged.GlobalWhat's next for Trump after Supreme Court crushed his tariffs?The Supreme Court struck down Trump's sweeping tariffs, prompting an angry response and fresh 10% levy plans, while critics warn the ruling deepens trade uncertainty ahead of crucial midterm elections.Load MoreAdvertisementAlso WatchUnPolitics: Milind Deora on Congress exit, Mahayuti role, 2029 South Mumbai bidPost-Mortem: Disturbing crimes rooted in obsession, emotional turmoil shock IndiaHealth 360: India's rare disease patients struggle while Rs 271 crore funds unspentAdvertisementLatest StoriesNation knows Congress already naked: PM Modi shreds party over shirtless protest at AI SummitUP was in fear earlier, now only development: PM Modi slams past governmentsCase against seer Avimukteshwaranand on charges of sexual assault on minor disciplesAdvertisement

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan tensions
description: Afghanistan criticised Pakistan for carrying out airstrikes on terror targets inside its territory that it said killed dozens of civilians, warning that repeated attacks by Islamabad would not conceal its internal crisis.

title: Hong Kong fire buildings
description: The government estimates that buying back the rights of some 1,700 units would cost about 6.8 billion Hong Kong dollars (USD 870 million), with about HK$4 billion Hong Kong (USD 512 million) of that sum to be from public funds and the rest to be covered by a relief fund.

title: Millions of Americans brace for blizzard conditions as storm threatens East Coast
description: The weather service said 1 to 2 feet (about 30 to 61 centimeters) of snow was possible in many areas as it issued blizzard warnings for New York City and Long Island, Boston and coastal communities in New Jersey, Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Rhode Island and Massachusetts. Flooding was also possible in parts of New York and New Jersey, it said.

title: Neal Katyal
description: Indian-origin lawyer Neal Katyal, who successfully challenged Donald Trump’s global tariffs in the US Supreme Court, has questioned the US President’s plan to use Section 122, saying the law lacks legal force in this case. Section 122 permits a US president to levy tariffs of up to 15% for a maximum of 150 days to tackle balance-of-payments deficits, after which approval from Congress is required.

title: Donald Trump lights a candle in a Diwali celebration in the Oval Office
description: Seven-in-ten Hindus (70 per cent) and 65 per cent of Jews have a bachelor's degree or more education; that compares with 35 per cent of US adults overall, according to the RLS study made public on February 19.

title: Mike Huckabee
description: Mike Huckabee's comments sparked immediate backlash from neighbouring Egypt and Jordan, Saudi Arabia, the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation and the League of Arab States, which in separate statements called them extremist, provocative and not in line with the US position.

title: Iran protests
description: A video purportedly showed rows of marchers at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology condemning Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as a "murderous leader", and calling for Reza Pahlavi, the exiled son of Iran's toppled shah, to be a new monarch.

title: Emmanuel Macron, Donald Trump
description: French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed the US Supreme Court’s decision striking down Donald Trump’s emergency tariffs, saying democracies need counterweights and rule of law.

title: Senior Hezbollah leader among 10 killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon
description: At least 10 people, including a senior Hezbollah leader, were killed in Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon after the Israel Defence Forces said it targeted Hezbollah positions.

title: The Fattah class of hypersonic missiles that can be used by Iran against US Navy carrier battle groups.
description: Iran News: Iran's Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei, stated the country possesses a dangerous weapon capable of sending US aircraft carriers to the bottom of the sea. While he did not mention the exact weapon, Iran does possess a vast arsenal of hypersonic and subsonic missiles that it could use against US warships, should need arise.

title: Neal Katyal
description: Neal Katyal, a former Acting Solicitor General of the US and author, has emerged as a central figure in the Supreme Court case that struck down Trump's global tariffs. Who is he?

title: Emmanuel Macron and Giorgia Meloni
description: A dispute has intensified between French President Emmanuel Macron and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni following the killing of French activist Quentin Deranque, drawing attention to growing ideological divides within Europe.

title: Trump given options to kill Khamenei and son in targeted Iran strikes: Report
description: US President Donald Trump has been briefed on military options that include a targeted strike against Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and his son Mojtaba Khamenei, Axios reported, citing senior administration sources.

title: The boy killed his father by shooting him in the head while he was sleeping.
description: An 11-year-old Pennsylvania boy shot his adoptive father in the head while he slept after his gaming console was taken away on the night of his birthday.

title: I can do anything I want: Defiant Trump after US Supreme Court tariff blow
description: President Donald Trump said he could do anything he wanted in his first reaction to a landmark ruling by the US Supreme Court on Friday that struck down his global tariffs, dealing a major blow to his second-term trade agenda.

title: Quentin Griffiths
description: Quentin Griffiths, 58, co-founder of British fashion retailer Asos, has died after falling from his 17th-floor apartment in Thailand's Pattaya on February 9. CCTV footage and an autopsy found no evidence of foul play, and police believe the death was a suicide.

title: US Supreme Court declared Trump tariffs unconstitutional in major blow to president
description: The US Supreme Court ruled 6-3 against President Trump’s tariffs under IEEPA. The verdict emphasises constitutional limits on presidential power and Congress’ role in taxation.

title: Donald Trump gestures as he speaks during a press briefing at the White House on Friday after tarrif verdict. (Photo: Reuters)
description: Certain members of the court are nothing but fools and lapdogs for the RINOs and the radical left,  deeply unpatriotic and disloyal to the Constitution, regardless of any other considerations, Trump alleged.

title: Donald Trump
description: The Supreme Court struck down Trump’s sweeping tariffs, prompting an angry response and fresh 10% levy plans, while critics warn the ruling deepens trade uncertainty ahead of crucial midterm elections.
